ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general okra dish class; no specific sambal okra entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific sambal-style okra dish code. A0CKP (okra, cooked) is the closest defensible anchor. Sambal seasoning and belacan are not captured in this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for sambal lady's finger or okra dish in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical side dish serving of sambal lady's finger is approximately 100g, equivalent to about 6–8 pieces of stir-fried okra with sambal sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Sambal Lady's Finger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo database for Sambal Lady's Finger (CR ID: F14080027). All per_100g macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from this record. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central okra and sambal component data as HPB does not report these sub-fractions for this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Okra and Vegetable Dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on cooked okra and low-GI vegetable dish data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sambal lady's finger. GI estimated at approximately 20 based on cooked okra (GI ~20, low category) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Okra's high soluble fibre (mucilage) content is well-documented to attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (20) × available carbohydrate per serving (7.2g per 100g serving) / 100 = 1.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 661mg per 100g — exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold due to belacan (fermented shrimp paste) and sambal paste
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit or avoid sambal lady's finger due to its high sodium content of 661mg/100g; request reduced belacan and limit portion to no more than 50g per meal to keep sodium contribution below 330mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~20) and high dietary fibre (4.7g/100g) from okra mucilage slow glucose absorption and attenuate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sambal lady's finger is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes given its low GI (~20), moderate carbohydrate (7.2g/100g), and high soluble fibre (4.7g/100g); however, the high fat content (9.8g/100g) from cooking oil should be moderated by requesting less oil to reduce caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (661mg/100g) is a cardiovascular risk factor; dietary fibre (4.7g/100g) and low saturated fat (~1.5g/100g) are protective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high sodium content (661mg/100g) from belacan poses a cardiovascular risk and patients should request reduced belacan; the dish's high dietary fibre (4.7g/100g) and low saturated fat (~1.5g/100g) are beneficial for lipid management and should be balanced against sodium exposure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory dietary fibre and vitamin C from okra are beneficial, but chilli (nightshade) and high sodium may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Okra provides anti-inflammatory fibre (4.7g/100g) and vitamin C (~15mg/100g), but patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should monitor tolerance to chilli in sambal, and should request reduced belacan to lower sodium (661mg/100g) which may worsen tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sambal Lady's Finger (Bendi Sambal) is a popular Malay and Singaporean side dish consisting of fresh okra (lady's finger) stir-fried in a wok with belacan (fermented shrimp paste block), dried or fresh chilli, garlic, and shallots. The dish is cooked over high heat in cooking oil until the okra is tender but retains a slight bite, coated in a fragrant, spicy, and umami-rich sambal sauce. The flavour profile is bold — intensely savoury from the belacan, moderately spicy from the chilli, with the characteristic slightly mucilaginous texture of okra. Per 100g, the dish provides 127 kcal, 2.5g protein, 9.8g fat, 7.2g carbohydrate, and a notably high dietary fibre content of 4.7g, primarily from okra's soluble mucilage. Sodium is high at 661mg/100g due to belacan, making it unsuitable for hypertension patients without modification. The low glycemic index (~20) and high fibre make it favourable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes. Calcium (76mg/100g) and iron (1.4mg/100g) contribute to micronutrient intake. The dish is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, but contains crustacean allergens from belacan. It is commonly served as a side dish with steamed rice in Malay, Singaporean, and Malaysian home cooking and hawker settings.
common_user_queries: Is sambal okra suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in sambal bendi?
Is sambal lady's finger high in fibre?

Does sambal lady's finger contain shrimp?

confidence_reason: Exact match found in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F14080027) with confidence 0.95. All core macronutrients and key micronutrients (calcium, iron, dietary fibre, sodium) are sourced directly from HPB. Saturated fat (~1.5g), sugar (~2.5g), potassium (~200mg), and vitamin C (~15mg) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central okra and sambal component data and are clearly documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database cooked okra data.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.5g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked okra and typical cooking oil composition; HPB does not report this sub-fraction for this entry. 2. Sugar (2.5g/100g) is estimated from carbohydrate composition of okra and sambal ingredients; HPB does not report sugar separately. 3. Potassium (~200mg/100g) and vitamin C (~15mg/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central okra data (USDA FDC ID 169260) and are not reported by HPB for this entry. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile for this sambal preparation not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. 5. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: no trans fats expected in a traditional stir-fry using standard cooking oil; confirmed negligible per USDA component data. 6. cholesterol_mg set to -1: belacan contains trace cholesterol from shrimp but composite dish value not reported in HPB or USDA for this preparation. 7. water_g and remaining micronutrients (vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium) set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry and no reliable composite dish data available in approved sources. 8. GI of ~20 is an analogous estimate based on cooked okra from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for sambal lady's finger exists in the published literature. 9. Dietary tag 'vegetarian' in the original input is incorrect — belacan contains fermented shrimp (crustacean); corrected to non-vegetarian and non-vegan in this record. 10. is_halal set to true as the dish is a traditional Malay preparation using halal-certified ingredients in standard preparation.
